Bridge to Terabithia (2007)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $82,234,139Details
International Box Office $55,750,649
Worldwide Box Office $137,984,788
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $44,104,337
Total $44,104,337
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Deep in the woods, far beyond the road, across a stream, lies a secret world only two people on Earth know about—a world brimming with fantastical creatures, glittering palaces and magical forests. This is Terabithia, where two young friends will discover how to rule their own kingdom, fight the forces of darkness and change their lives forever through the power of the imagination.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$22,564,612 (27.4% of total gross)
Legs:3.64 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:59.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$25,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,139 opening theaters/3,210 max. theaters, 6.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $142,953,527

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: February 16th, 2007 (Wide) by Walt Disney
Video Release: June 19th, 2007 by Walt Disney Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for thematic elements including bullying, some peril and mild language.
Running Time: 94 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Fantasy Sequence
Plot point: Coming of Age, New Guy/Girl in School
Subgenre: Coming-of-Age Drama
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Walt Disney Pictures, Walden Media, Hal Lieberman Company, Lauren Levine
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Deep in the woods, far beyond the road, across a stream, lies a secret world only two people on Earth know about—a world brimming with fantastical creatures, glittering palaces and magical forests. This is Terabithia, where two young friends will discover how to rule their own kingdom, fight the forces of darkness and change their lives forever through the power of the imagination.
